1895	// TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure verifies that when HeadRev fails after the agent
1896	// runs, the outcome is "error" and the reason contains the original error message.
1897	// This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go lines 282-309.
1898	func TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
1899		t.Run("json output", func(t *testing.T) {
1900			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1901				mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1902				wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("disk read error"),
1903			}
1904			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1905			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1906	
1907			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1908			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
1909			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1910			res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1911	
1912			assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1913			assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1914			assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "disk read error")
1915			assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1916		})
1917	
1918		t.Run("text output", func(t *testing.T) {
1919			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1920				mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1921				wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("disk read error"),
1922			}
1923			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1924			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1925	
1926			root := f.NewRootCommand()
1927			out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead")
1928			require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1929	
1930			assert.Contains(t, out, "outcome: error")
1931			assert.Contains(t, out, "disk read error")
1932		})
1933	}

1935	// TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure verifies that when the
1936	// agent runner returns an error AND HeadRev fails on the worktree, both the
1937	// Error field (agent message) and the Reason field (rev error) are present in
1938	// the JSON output. This covers the path at agent_execute_bead.go that
1939	// previously dropped the agent error message when revErr was non-nil.
1940	func TestExecuteBeadCompoundErrorAgentAndHeadRevFailure(t *testing.T) {
1941		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1942			mainHeadRev:  "aaaa1111",
1943			wtHeadRevErr: fmt.Errorf("worktree HEAD unreadable"),
1944		}
1945		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent exploded"), result: nil}
1946		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1947	
1948		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1949		out, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
1950		require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1951		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1952	
1953		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1954		assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1955		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1956		assert.Equal(t, "agent exploded", res.Error,
1957			"agent error message must be preserved even when HeadRev also fails")
1958		assert.Contains(t, res.Reason, "worktree HEAD unreadable",
1959			"Reason must reflect the HeadRev failure")
1960	}
1961	
1962	// TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID verifies that beadIDs with characters illegal
1963	// in git ref names are rejected with a clear error before any git or agent
1964	// operations are attempted.
1965	func TestExecuteBeadInvalidBeadID(t *testing.T) {
1966		invalidIDs := []string{
1967			"bead with spaces",
1968			"bead~1",
1969			"bead^1",
1970			"bead:name",
1971			"bead[0]",
1972		}
1973		for _, id := range invalidIDs {
1974			t.Run(id, func(t *testing.T) {
1975				git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111"}
1976				runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1977				f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1978	
1979				root := f.NewRootCommand()
1980				_, cmdErr := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", id)
1981				require.Error(t, cmdErr)
1982				assert.Contains(t, cmdErr.Error(), "invalid bead ID")
1983	
1984				// No git or agent operations should have been attempted.
1985				assert.Empty(t, git.addedWTs, "no worktree should be created for invalid bead ID")
1986			})
1987		}
1988	}

1990	// TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields verifies that runtime evidence fields are
1991	// populated in the JSON output.
1992	func TestExecuteBeadEvidenceFields(t *testing.T) {
1993		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1994			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1995			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
1996		}
1997		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
1998			ExitCode: 0,
1999			Harness:  "testharness",
2000			Model:    "test-model",
2001			Tokens:   42,
2002			CostUSD:  0.001,
2003		}}
2004		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2005	
2006		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
2007	
2008		assert.Equal(t, "testharness", res.Harness)
2009		assert.Equal(t, "test-model", res.Model)
2010		assert.Equal(t, 42, res.Tokens)
2011		assert.InDelta(t, 0.001, res.CostUSD, 1e-9)
2012		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
2013		assert.False(t, res.StartedAt.IsZero())
2014		assert.False(t, res.FinishedAt.IsZero())
2015		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
2016		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
2017	}

2019	// TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough locks in the resolution contract for
2020	// execute-bead's model option: the value supplied via ExecuteBeadOptions.Model
2021	// is passed verbatim to the runner, and an empty value is not silently replaced
2022	// by any hardcoded or catalog-derived default. This regression test guards
2023	// against routing layers injecting a model (e.g. a stale vendor/model like
2024	// "z-ai/glm-5.1") when the caller did not request one — the case the agent
2025	// harness resolves from ~/.config/agent/config.yaml must be preserved by
2026	// ExecuteBead handing the runner an empty Model so the harness's own
2027	// resolution chain runs.
2028	func TestExecuteBeadModelFlagPassthrough(t *testing.T) {
2029		t.Run("empty model stays empty through ExecuteBead", func(t *testing.T) {
2030			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2031				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2032				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2033			}
2034			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2035			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2036	
2037			// No --model flag supplied to execute-bead.
2038			runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
2039	
2040			assert.Equal(t, "", runner.last.Model,
2041				"runner must receive an empty Model when no --model flag is provided; "+
2042					"any non-empty value here indicates a routing layer injected a default, "+
2043					"which would override the harness's own config-driven resolution")
2044		})
2045	
2046		t.Run("explicit model is forwarded verbatim", func(t *testing.T) {
2047			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2048				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
2049				wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
2050			}
2051			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
2052			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2053	
2054			runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--model", "qwen3.5-27b")
2055	
2056			assert.Equal(t, "qwen3.5-27b", runner.last.Model,
2057				"runner must receive the exact --model value the caller passed")
2058		})
2059	}

1827	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput verifies that when the agent runner
1828	// returns an error, the error message appears in the JSON output Error field.
1829	// Exercises real git + RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for
1830	// the components under test.
1831	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorMessageInOutput(t *testing.T) {
1832		workDir := t.TempDir()
1833	
1834		scrubEnv := func() []string {
1835			parent := os.Environ()
1836			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
1837			for _, kv := range parent {
1838				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
1839					continue
1840				}
1841				env = append(env, kv)
1842			}
1843			return env
1844		}
1845		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1846			t.Helper()
1847			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1848			cmd.Dir = workDir
1849			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1850			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1851			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1852			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1853		}
1854	
1855		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1856		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1857		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1858		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1859		runGit("add", ".")
1860		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1861	
1862		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1863			ID:        "my-bead",
1864			Title:     "Test execute-bead agent error message in output",
1865			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1866			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1867		})
1868		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1869		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1870		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1871	
1872		// Script harness: fail immediately on the first directive so the runner
1873		// returns (result, execErr) with no commits made. Mirrors the original
1874		// fakeAgentRunner{err: ..., result: nil} with no commits.
1875		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1876		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
1877			"fail-during 0\n",
1878		), 0o644))
1879	
1880		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1881		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1882		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1883	
1884		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1885		out, _ := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1886			"--from", baseSHA,
1887			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1888		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1889	
1890		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1891		// Agent runner's error message must appear in the JSON Error field.
1892		assert.Equal(t, "script harness: synthetic failure at directive 0", res.Error)
1893	}
1894

760			_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res)

764		// Write usage.json when the harness reports token usage or cost.
765		// Done before SynthesizeCommit so usage data is available in the
766		// preliminary result written for commit-message sourcing.

789			if isDirty, _ := gitOps.IsDirty(wtPath); isDirty {
790				// Build a preliminary result and write it to result.json before
791				// calling SynthesizeCommit. The commit message is then sourced from
792				// the tracked artifact file, satisfying the provenance contract:
793				// "commit-message metadata must be projected from tracked artifact
794				// files, never from ad hoc runtime state" (FEAT-006).
795				// The final result.json is re-written after the commit with the
796				// correct ResultRev, Outcome, and Status.
797				prelimOutcome := ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded
798				if exitCode != 0 {
799					prelimOutcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
800				}
801				prelimRes := &ExecuteBeadResult{
802					BeadID:       beadID,
803					AttemptID:    attemptID,
804					WorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,
805					BaseRev:      baseRev,
806					ResultRev:    "", // unknown until commit is made
807					Harness:      resultHarness,
808					Provider:     resultProvider,
809					Model:        resultModel,
810					SessionID:    sessionID,
811					DurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),
812					Tokens:       tokens,
813					CostUSD:      costUSD,
814					ExitCode:     exitCode,
815					Error:        agentErrMsg,
816					ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
817					PromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,
818					ManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,
819					ResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,
820					UsageFile:    usageFileRel,
821					StartedAt:    startedAt,
822					FinishedAt:   finishedAt,
823					Outcome:      prelimOutcome,
824				}
825				populateWorkerStatus(prelimRes)
826				_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, prelimRes)
827	
828				// Render the commit message from the tracked artifact file.
829				commitMsg, msgErr := BuildCommitMessageFromResultFile(artifacts.ResultAbs)

835					if newRev, _ := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath); newRev != baseRev {

164	// LandBeadResult is the parent-side orchestrator. It receives a completed worker
165	// result and decides whether to merge, preserve, or report no-changes. All
166	// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, and preserve-ref management live here.
167	// The worker (ExecuteBead) must not call any of these operations.
168	//
169	// Outcome rules:
170	//   - ResultRev == BaseRev → "no-changes" (agent made no commits)
171	//   - ExitCode != 0 with commits → "preserved" (agent failed but left output)
172	//   - Gate failed → "preserved"
173	//   - NoMerge → "preserved"
174	//   - Default → attempt merge; on conflict → "preserved"
175	func LandBeadResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, gitOps OrchestratorGitOps, opts BeadLandingOptions) (*BeadLandingResult, error) {
176		landing := &BeadLandingResult{}
177	
178		// Agent failed with no commits: report as error (not no-changes).
179		// Prefer res.Reason (e.g. HeadRev failure set by worker) over res.Error
180		// (agent error) so the primary context failure is surfaced as the reason.
181		if res.ExitCode != 0 && res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
182			landing.Outcome = "error"
183			switch {
184			case res.Reason != "":
185				landing.Reason = res.Reason
186			case res.Error != "":
187				landing.Reason = res.Error
188			default:
189				landing.Reason = "agent execution failed"
190			}
191			return landing, nil
192		}
193	
194		// No changes from the worker: nothing to land.
195		if res.ResultRev == res.BaseRev {
196			landing.Outcome = "no-changes"
197			if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
198				landing.Reason = res.NoChangesRationale
199			} else {

23	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26

[detached HEAD 2140ed4] test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure to real git + script harness [ddx-7f9a7009]

data.text: Migrated `TestExecuteBeadHeadRevFailure` to real git + script harness. Both subtests now seed a real repo, then drive `ExecuteBead` end-to-end via the script harness with a `delete-file .git` directive that corrupts the worktree's git pointer — so `git rev-parse HEAD` against the worktree fails for real after the agent returns success. Asserts updated to look for `rev-parse` in the reason. All other tests in the file untouched. Tests green; commit `2140ed4` landed.
